macro_events.event_summary: Oil prices spike 3% after Iran retaliates against a US airbase following fresh American strikes, reviving Strait of Hormuz disruption fears.
primary_drivers: Crude price spike lifts upstream E&P realizations
Geopolitical risk premium on Middle East shipping/Strait of Hormuz transit
Defense procurement and missile-defense demand acceleration

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream exposure; ~40%+ of earnings leverage to Brent. Direct beneficiary of higher crude realizations and minimal Iranian asset exposure.
Highly oil-weighted Permian pure-play E&P with high operating leverage to WTI; every $5 WTI move materially shifts FCF. No Middle East operating risk.
E&P with high beta to oil price swings and explicit geopolitical-risk correlation in its trading pattern; smaller-cap = larger % move per dollar of oil.
Maker of THAAD, PAC-3 Patriot missiles and F-35 — the exact systems being used/replenished in airbase defense. Direct procurement tailwind from Iran kinetic exchange.
Raytheon makes Patriot interceptors and SM-series missiles deployed in CENTCOM; missile-defense reload demand is the textbook trade on Iranian missile strikes against US bases.
TechnipFMC — offshore/subsea services provider benefits from sustained crude risk premium driving accelerated non-OPEC, non-Gulf project FIDs.
Scorpio Tankers — product tanker rates spike when Hormuz transit risk forces rerouting and ton-mile expansion; clean-product tanker pure-play.

losers.rationale: Delta has the highest jet fuel cost exposure as % of opex among US majors and limited hedging in recent quarters; 3% crude move flows directly to next-quarter unit costs.
United has substantial transpacific/transatlantic routing that may need to avoid Iranian airspace, plus fuel cost passthrough lag. Double hit.
Southwest is unhedged on fuel and runs thin margins; crude spike is direct EPS hit with no offset.
Carnival — bunker fuel is a major opex line and Middle East/Red Sea itineraries face cancellation risk; already pressured by prior Houthi-related reroutes.
FedEx air freight is heavily jet-fuel exposed and surcharge passthrough lags by a quarter; also Middle East air corridor disruption affects Asia-Europe network.
Dow Chemical's ethylene cracker feedstock and energy costs rise with crude; commodity chemicals have weak passthrough in current demand environment.

headlines: Oil jumps 3% after Iran says it targeted U.S. airbase following fresh American strikes
